enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — the canonical representation of the VCF must be identical after shuffling meta-information lines (except ##fileformat which stays first).

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line key=value pair order invariance
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — reordering key=value pairs inside ##INFO, ##FORMAT, or ##FILTER structured lines must not change the parsed semantics.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.oracle: A multi-ALT record (e.g., chr1 100 A T,C) split into individual records (chr1 100 A T and chr1 100 A C) must represent the same set of variants. Number=A INFO/FORMAT arrays must be correctly partitioned, and GT indices must be remapped to preserve REF (0) semantics.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
ALT --- alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles.

enforced.mr_name: Fileformat first-line violation must be rejected
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a VCF file where ##fileformat is not the first line. Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
A single `fileformat' line is always required, must be the first line in the file, and details the VCF format version number.

enforced.mr_name: Missing mandatory fixed column must be rejected
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a VCF data line missing one of the 8 mandatory fixed columns (CHROM, POS, ID, REF, ALT, QUAL, FILTER, INFO). Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: All data lines are tab-delimited with no tab character at the end of the line.
We state explicitly that zero length strings are not allowed, this includes the CHROM and ID column, INFO IDs, FILTER IDs and FORMAT IDs.

enforced.mr_name: GT allele index out of bounds must be rejected
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a VCF record where a GT allele index exceeds len(ALT). Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: The allele values are 0 for the reference allele (what is in the REF field), 1 for the first allele listed in ALT, 2 for the second allele list in ALT and so on.

enforced.mr_name: INFO Number=A cardinality mismatch must be rejected
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a VCF record where an INFO field declared Number=A has a value count different from len(ALT). Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines).

enforced.mr_name: POS-END coordinate interval invariance under meta-line shuffle
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getStart, getEnd, getLengthOnReference, getChr, getContig must be identical after shuffling meta-lines since these are data-level properties independent of header ordering

evidence.quote: POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.
This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele

enforced.mr_name: POS-END coordinate interval invariance under sample column permutation
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getStart, getEnd, getLengthOnReference, getChr, getContig must be identical after permuting sample columns since coordinates are sample-independent fixed-column properties

evidence.quote: POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.
This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele

enforced.mr_name: POS-END coordinate interval invariance under INFO field key-value shuffle
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getStart, getEnd, getLengthOnReference, getChr, getContig must be identical after shuffling INFO key-value order since coordinates are in fixed columns, not INFO

evidence.quote: POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.
This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele

enforced.mr_name: POS-END coordinate interval invariance under VCF-BCF round-trip
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getStart, getEnd, getLengthOnReference, getChr, getContig must be identical after VCF->BCF->VCF round-trip since coordinates are preserved in binary encoding per VCF v4.5 §6

evidence.quote: POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.
This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele

enforced.mr_name: POS-END coordinate interval invariance under write round-trip
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getStart, getEnd, getLengthOnReference, getChr, getContig must be identical after write round-trip since coordinates are fundamental record properties preserved through serialization

evidence.quote: POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.
This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele

enforced.oracle: Parsing the original VCF and parsing the VCF obtained after BCF round-trip must yield identical canonical content (same records, same INFO/FORMAT values, same GT genotypes).

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — BCF specification] Overall, the idea behind is BCF2 is simple. BCF2 is a binary, compressed equivalent of VCF that can be indexed with tabix and can be efficiently decoded from disk or streams.
[VCF v4.5 — BCF specification] The BCF2 encoder / decoder must automatically add and remove these END_OF_VECTOR values from the vectors.

enforced.mr_name: Missing-value round-trip via equivalent FORMAT field injection
enforced.oracle: Appending a FORMAT field filled with missing values ('.') to all samples must not change the semantic content of the VCF; a spec-compliant parser must produce identical canonical output (missing values are semantically equivalent to absent data).

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — The VCF specification] If unknown, the MISSING value must be specified.
[VCF v4.5 — BCF specification] Note that the use of END_OF_VECTOR means that it is legal to encode a vector VCF field with MISSING values.

enforced.mr_name: Meta-line shuffle preserves query method results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method. Shuffling meta-information lines (other than ##fileformat) is semantically a no-op per VCF v4.5 §Meta-information lines, so all public query methods on parsed records must return identical values.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line key-value reorder preserves query results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method. Reordering key=value pairs inside structured ##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER meta-lines must not change parser behavior, as implementations must not rely on field ordering.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — BCF dictionary entries (##contig, ##INFO, ##FORMAT, ##FILTER) can be declared in any order; the BCF codec re-indexes them, so round-tripping through BCF must produce identical canonical output.

evidence.quote: It is recommended to respect the ordering as specified in the input VCF/BCF2 file, but parsers should not rely on a specific ordering.

ambiguity_flags: ADVISORY severity — the spec recommends respecting input order but says parsers should not rely on it; a strict parser might still preserve original order.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — the order of sample columns in the VCF is not semantically meaningful; permuting them must produce identical canonical output when samples are identified by their IDs.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly address sample column ordering; the MR relies on the principle that sample identity is by ID, not column position.

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key=value order invariance in data lines
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — the semicolon-separated key=value pairs in the INFO column have no specified ordering requirement; shuffling them must produce identical canonical output.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

ambiguity_flags: The spec explicitly addresses structured meta-line field ordering but does not explicitly address INFO key=value ordering in data lines; the MR extends the same principle to data-line INFO fields.

quarantine.mr_name: ALT permutation with Number=A and Number=R field remapping
preconditions: header_has_info_number_a OR header_has_format_number_a OR header_has_info_number_r OR header_has_format_number_r
quarantine.oracle: After permuting ALT alleles and remapping Number=A and Number=R fields accordingly, the parsed record must produce identical results for all allele-independent queries (getNAlleles, getType, isVariant, etc.) and the remapped Number=A/R values must correspond to the new ALT order.

evidence.quote:
* A: The field has one value per alternate allele.
  The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).

* R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference.
  The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
* ALT — alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles.

quarantine.mr_name: ALT permutation with query method invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — getNAlleles, isBiallelic, isVariant, isSNP, isIndel, getType, and hasGenotypes must all be invariant under ALT permutation with consistent remapping.

evidence.quote:
* A: The field has one value per alternate allele.
  The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).

* R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference.
  The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
* ALT — alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles.

quarantine.oracle: The canonical normalized representation (parsimonious, left-anchored) of the variant must be semantically equivalent before and after trimming. A spec-compliant parser must recognize REF=AA,ALT=AC at POS=100 as the same variant as REF=A,ALT=C at POS=101.

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.
ALT --- alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says padding base is 'not required (although it is permitted)' for complex substitutions — this MAY/SHOULD ambiguity means both padded and unpadded forms are valid, making normalization invariance a test of parser equivalence across equivalent representations.

quarantine.oracle: A left-shifted indel in a homopolymer run (e.g., deletion of A in AAAA at POS=101 -> POS=100) must be recognized as the same variant by a spec-compliant parser. The canonical representation per Tan 2015 §2.1 requires left-alignment.

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly mandate left-alignment of indels in homopolymer runs; it only describes the padding base rule. Left-alignment is a best practice from Tan 2015 but not a VCF spec requirement per se.

quarantine.oracle: Parsing a VCF file with the primary SUT, then re-serializing via its writer API, then re-parsing must produce identical canonical JSON. Any diff exposes a writer bug in the primary SUT (Chen, Kuo, Liu, Tse 2018 §3.2).

evidence.quote: REF — reference base(s): Each base must be one of A,C,G,T,N (case insensitive). Multiple bases are permitted. The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.
ALT --- alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles.

ambiguity_flags: Tools processing VCF files are not required to preserve case in the allele String, except for IDs, which are case sensitive — this MAY introduce case normalization differences across round-trips.
